name.markdownDescription:
Logical name of this input

Sets a logical name (e.g., 'myInput') for this input. The [inputName](https://doc.log10x.com/api/js/#TenXBaseObject+inputName) field  returns this value at run time to allow for identifying and operating on instances originating from this input.

path.markdownDescription:
A file path or glob pattern to read from

Specifies a path that is either an absolute or canonical file path. For example: ``` yaml path: $=path("<user.dir>/data/sample/[!{.}]*.log") ``` (Default: path("<user.dir>/data/sample/[!{.}]*.log"))

enabled.markdownDescription:
Enable/disable this file input

Specifies whether the open the input to read events from the target [inputFilePath](https://doc.log10x.com/run/input/file/#inputfilepath). (Accepts boolean or string with $= prefix for runtime evaluation) (Default: true)

extractors.markdownDescription:
JSON / regex extractor names

Defines a list of JSON/regex extractor names to capture events from 'inputFilePath' to transform into TenXObjects. Each item in the list should map to an extractor defined via a configuration file or startup arguments group. To learn more see: [extractors](https://doc.log10x.com/run/input/extract).

sourcePattern.markdownDescription:
Event source capture regex pattern

Defines a regex pattern to capture 'source' values for each event read from this input. Source values provide a method for sequencing TenXObjects read from this input in separate logical queues.  For example, capturing a 'host' or 'fileName' value as a 'source' creates a separate logical queue for  each unique file/host origin by which TenXObjects read from this input are aggregated and written to output.  The [source](https://doc.log10x.com/api/js/#TenXObject+source) member returns each TenXObject's assigned value at run time. .

sourceFields.markdownDescription:
Event source JSON fields

Defines a list of JSON fields to extract from matching capture groups of [inputFileSourcePattern](https://doc.log10x.com/run/input/file/#inputfilesourcepattern) or the entire event if not set as the 'source' value for each event read from this input.  Source values provide a method for sequencing TenXObjects read from this input in separate logical queues.  For example, capturing a 'host' or 'fileName' value as a 'source' creates a separate logical queue for  each unique file/host origin by which TenXObjects read from this input are aggregated and written to output.  The [source](https://doc.log10x.com/api/js/#TenXObject+source) member returns each TenXObject's assigned value at run time.

printProgress.markdownDescription:
Sets whether this input prints throughput stats to the console

Sets whether this input prints throughput stats to the console (Accepts boolean or string with $= prefix for runtime evaluation)
